working_tree: Benchmark executable and reports correspond to the implementation committed as c3ea2ee7036b7d25633a0685f2d00bd46039ae74; documentation/evidence files were added afterward.
protocol: Each profile generated a fresh DEFLATE level 6 source workbook in a separate process, ran two warm-ups, retained five measured editor-process runs and selected the elapsed-time median representative. Only targeted upsert was measured.
peak_memory_metric: Windows PROCESS_MEMORY_COUNTERS.PeakWorkingSetSize for each benchmark process; source fixture generation and Python verification are outside measured reused-source runs.
transform_metrics: Single-pass transform microseconds, parsed/source-callback/coalesced/action traffic, output append/flush counts, peak owned batching buffer, relationship scan time, temporary-file write time and the source-scan/action residual.
package_writer_metrics: Caller-owned internal telemetry enabled only for benchmark save: package and target worksheet entry open/read/write/close timing, bytes and call counts. Normal callers do not collect per-call clock data.
compatibility_protocol: The runner validated output-plan byte accounting and exact compressed payload equality for every raw-copy entry in every warm-up and measured output. Each median representative opened with openpyxl 3.1.5. The relationship-bearing formula profile additionally streamed the worksheet XML and inspected the small worksheet relationship part to verify the A1 external hyperlink binding and target without loading a one-million-cell DOM.
scope: Single Windows/MSVC machine, numeric/mixed-inline/mixed-shared/formula source distributions, one relationship-bearing fixture, one-million and five-million source-cell profiles, 1,000 targeted upserts and output DEFLATE level 1. No arbitrary-workbook, random-editing, cross-machine or Office compatibility claim.
